opp.name: CS Energy_S2P_2026
opp.next_step: 50% Confidence 19 JUN - Working on connecting with Jeff Green, have emailed and he has accepted LinkedIn. Pushed deal out for now. 9th Jun Plan to connect Andre Harvey, once relationship established- targeting week of 15th Jun 25 MAY - New CPO, Jeff Brown, is onboard. Working to get in front of. 31 MAR - New CPO starts mid April and team will present requirements / plans to him for decision to proceed 13 MAR - waiting on these example use cases and the new CPO starting 04 MAR - we're still waiting on use cases. new CPO onboarding in April 05 FEB - NDA executed, they will now send us 'example use cases' to demo back to senior Proc team 03 FEB - still waiting on NDA, have followed up. Am connecting CPO with Andre Harvey also. 21 JAN - Good discovery meeting, S2C opportunity confirmed targeting Q2. Next step is a use case demo session and they are sending examples plus NDA to get started. 19 JAN - In person discovery meeting with CS Energy tomorrow 05 JAN - In person discovery meeting planned for 20 January with procurement tech project lead. 18 DEC - Connected with Dee Agarwal, project planned for 2026. In-person discovery scheduled for 20 Jan. 28 NOV - Caryn Ochtman advised she should have an update on their 2026 plans prior to Christmas. Luke met Phillp Naharung(HOP) at an event and told that there is no immediate project so should follow up
opp.ais_why:
• On Mar 24 2026, Caryn Ochtman (HOP) said in her catch-up with Luke Dougherty: 'we just seem to be at the moment quite flustered with a gazillion things that we need to to to do, and... our our headspace to actually deal with this particular requirement has actually keeps getting pushed.' On the same call Luke (rep) offered: 'we can give Jeff a bit of a look at our capability and what we're doing in the AI powered procurement space.' Buyer is disengaged; AI is being pushed by rep, not pulled by buyer.
• Per the 31 Mar 2026 rep note from Luke Dougherty: 'New CPO starts mid April and team will present requirements / plans to him for decision to proceed.' Earlier 5 Feb: 'NDA executed, they will now send us example use cases to demo back to senior Proc team.' Buyer language is all about CPO transition and example use cases - never AI, Merlin, or Agentic AI by name from a CS Energy stakeholder.
• High confidence: 3 Avoma meetings (csenergy.com.au confirmed), full Mar 24 transcript reviewed - Caryn Ochtman never spoke AI/Merlin/agentic-related words. Products in scope include Merlin + Agentic AI + Merlin Intake, but the buyer's stance is 'pushed/flustered' not enthusiastic. Stage is Qualified with new-CPO transition gating - AI Curious at the low end.

items.area: Buyer committed to provide example use cases for demo (2026-02-05), never delivered; no follow-up or re-engagement

items.area: Zycus provided 200-function requirements template; buyer has not completed it

items.area: New CPO (Jeff Green) start date and engagement status unknown; no confirmation he has onboarded or received briefing

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c
SF Compelling_Event__c
Avoma call 2026-03-24, Caryn Ochtman (buyer) 'flustered with a gazillion things,' 'time poor'
metrics.narrative: CS Energy articulated four operational pain points in Customer_Business_Problem__c: manual procurement processes causing inefficiencies and error risk; compliance and governance gaps creating audit exposure; inability to track supplier sustainability for ESG alignment; and data fragmentation with security risk from legacy systems. However, none of these have been quantified with cost, headcount waste, cycle-time impact, or savings opportunity. On the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn Ochtman described the team as 'flustered with a gazillion things' and 'time poor,' confirming resource strain but offering no dollar figure. Compelling_Event__c mentions 'ROI justification' as a requirement but provides no baseline numbers. The deal sits in Qualified nearly a year (349 days) with comprehensive pain articulation but zero financial metrics, blocking a defensible business case. The next move is to secure a quantified pain and ROI conversation with the new CPO once onboarded, anchoring the business case to measurable impact (cost avoidance, FTE productivity, compliance risk reduction) before advancing to evaluation.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Caryn Ochtman, Dee Agarwal, Allistair Johnston (no Champion role assigned)
SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c (null)

Avoma call 2026-03-24, Caryn Ochtman (buyer): 'flustered,' 'the ultimate decision most probably will end up landing in [the CPO's] particular square space,' 'I would want to be involved in that direction'
SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-19: rep reached out to CPO (Jeff Green), LinkedIn accepted, no substantive reply
champion.narrative: Caryn Ochtman (Senior Manager Procurement Governance & Technology) is the closest to a champion but her engagement has cooled and her influence is constrained by the CPO transition. She initiated contact in late 2025, led the 2026-01-20 discovery meeting, drove NDA execution in February 2026, and held the 2026-03-24 check-in where she transparently explained the deal stall: 'we've been so smashed with other things that our headspace to actually deal with this particular requirement has actually keeps getting pushed.' She acknowledged the procurement team would draft requirements internally and present to the new CPO, stating 'the ultimate decision most probably will end up landing in their particular square space.' This positions her as an operational advocate but NOT the decision maker. She has not introduced Zycus to the CPO, has not driven internal urgency, and has not responded to the deal owner's June 2026 outreach. Her last contact was 103 days ago (2026-03-24). Dee Agarwal (Snr Contracts and Procurement Manager) has been mentioned in tasks but has not appeared on a recorded call and shows no evidence of active advocacy. No champion role is assigned in OpportunityContactRoles. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c is null. The CPO (Jeff Green from the relevant stakeholder) is the unknown variable: if he inherits the project favorably and Caryn/Dee position Zycus well internally, he could become the champion; if he deprioritizes or redirects, the deal is at severe risk. Champion status is developing-to-passive with Caryn, and unknown with the CPO. Securing an introduction to the CPO and assessing his stance is the critical next move.

sources: SF Competitors__c (null), Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c (null), How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (null), Existing_vendor__c (null)
SF Customer_Business_Problem__c: 'operating without a formal procurement system' (greenfield signal)
Avoma calls, Next_Step_History__c, Tasks: no competitor mentioned
No RFP or RFI process evidenced
competition.narrative: No competitor has been named or evidenced across any source. Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are null. How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c (current solution/stack) is null. Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, and Moved_To__c are null. Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c, and Closed_Lost_Reason_Code__c are null. No competitor was mentioned on the 2026-03-24 call, in any Next Step entry, or in any Task description. The only vendor comparison context is implicit: CS Energy is 'operating without a formal procurement system' per Customer_Business_Problem__c, suggesting greenfield (no incumbent S2P platform to displace). However, 'no named competitor' in a 349-day-old Qualified deal is a red flag, not a green light. For a government-owned utilities entity exploring a major S2P transformation with no competitive intelligence, the most likely scenario is: (a) the buyer has not yet run a market scan or RFP process (consistent with 'requirements not finalized'), or (b) they are evaluating internally or with other vendors but have not disclosed it to the relevant stakeholder synthesis read shows zero competitive signal, which for THIS deal likely means early-stage / pre-competitive rather than sole-sourced. The move is to surface the competitive landscape proactively once the CPO engages, positioning Zycus's AI-powered S2P differentiation (Merlin negotiation agents, Agentic AI, recent SEQWater go-live as a Queensland peer reference) before a formal RFP opens the field.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c
SF Compelling_Event__c

Avoma call 2026-03-24, Caryn Ochtman (buyer): 'flustered with a gazillion things,' 'time poor,' 'government requests force reprioritization,' 'our headspace to actually deal with this particular requirement has actually keeps getting pushed'
identify_pain.narrative: CS Energy's procurement pain is well-documented and confirmed by the sponsor. Customer_Business_Problem__c articulates four operational pain points: (1) outdated procurement processes with manual workflows causing inefficiencies, high error risk, and poor spend visibility; (2) compliance and governance gaps creating audit exposure and policy-breach risk; (3) inability to track supplier sustainability practices and measure ESG impact, misaligning with environmental/social goals; (4) data fragmentation and security risk from legacy systems. Compelling_Event__c adds that CS Energy is 'actively exploring a modern procurement solution to replace manual, fragmented processes' as part of 'broader digital transformation goals.' On the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn Ochtman (Senior Manager Procurement Governance & Technology) described the team as 'quite flustered with a gazillion things that we need to do' and 'time poor,' and explained that 'government requests' force sudden reprioritization, parking ongoing work. She stated 'our headspace to actually deal with this particular requirement has actually keeps getting pushed,' confirming resource strain and competing priorities as immediate pain. The pain owner is the procurement leadership team (Caryn, Dee Agarwal, Allistair Johnston), with the new CPO inheriting ownership upon onboarding. Pain is confirmed and severe (manual processes, compliance risk, resource overload, no formal system), but not yet quantified with financial impact or tied to a forcing event with a hard deadline.

sources: SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c (null), Mandate__c (null)
SF Next_Step_History__c: NDA executed 2026-02-05
Avoma call 2026-03-24, Caryn Ochtman (buyer): 'joys of working for a GOC' (public-sector entity)
paper_process.narrative: No paper process or contracting mechanics have been discussed. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null (no incumbent contract forcing function). Mandate__c is null. NDA was executed 2026-02-05 per Next_Step_History__c, indicating basic legal clearing, but no MSA, SOW, or procurement contracting framework has been mentioned. CS Energy is a Queensland Government-Owned Corporation (GOC), and on the 2026-03-24 call the relevant stakeholder referenced 'the joys of working for a GOC' when explaining how government requests force reprioritization, suggesting procurement may be subject to public-sector governance, probity requirements, or tender processes. No InfoSec review, ERP/system integration scoping, or legal redlining discussion has occurred. Products in scope include iContract, iSource, iSave, iSupplier, iSaaS, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, and Agentic AI (a broad S2P suite), but no implementation partner or services scope has been defined. The deal is pre-commercial; paper-process visibility depends entirely on advancing to a formal evaluation and engaging the CPO on procurement governance and contracting requirements.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all null. No contact role carries the 'Economic Buyer' or 'Executive Sponsor' designation. The three known contacts are all procurement/tech operational roles: Caryn Ochtman (Senior Manager Procurement Governance & Technology), Dee Agarwal (Snr Contracts and Procurement Manager), Allistair Johnston (Technology Commercial Manager). On the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn referenced a new 'Head of Strategic Sourcing and Supply' (Jeff Green from the relevant stakeholder) joining mid-April 2026 and stated: 'the ultimate decision most probably will end up landing in their particular square space... I would want to be involved in that direction.' This positions Jeff Green as the likely Decision Maker for project approval, but his level of budget authority is unconfirmed. Compelling_Event__c states 'no budget is currently allocated' as of the field's last edit. No C-level sponsor (CFO, CPO, or procurement executive with P&L authority) has appeared on any call or task. The synthesis read shows budget authority resides with the incoming CPO, but that person has not yet engaged, and no conversation has occurred about approval process, spend threshold, or commercial timeline. Mapping and engaging the Economic Buyer is the single highest-priority move once the CPO onboards.

sources: SF What_is_the_decision_process__c (null), Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c (null)
SF Next_Step_History__c: NDA executed 2026-02-05, buyer to send use cases (never delivered)

Avoma call 2026-03-24, Caryn Ochtman (buyer): 'New CPO starts mid April and team will present requirements / plans to him for decision to proceed,' 'what have I inherited, and where do I want to take this particular team'
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is gated by CPO transition and has not been formally mapped. What_is_the_decision_process__c is null. The call sequence suggests: initial contact with the relevant stakeholder (procurement governance lead) and Dee Agarwal (contracts/procurement manager) in late 2025; discovery meeting scheduled 2026-01-20 with Allistair Johnston (tech commercial manager) added to the stakeholder set; NDA executed 2026-02-05; buyer committed to provide example use cases for a demo session but never delivered. On 2026-03-24, Caryn stated: 'New CPO starts mid April and team will present requirements / plans to him for decision to proceed.' She clarified the procurement leadership team (herself, Dee, likely Allistair) would draft requirements internally, then the CPO would 'contemplate, well, you know, what have I inherited, and where do I want to take this particular team on their journey' and make the final call. This positions the CPO as the approval gate. Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c and Multiple_approvals__c are null, so the approval chain above the CPO (CFO, board, government oversight as a GOC) is unknown. No timeline for decision has been set post-CPO onboarding. The process is effectively paused until the CPO engages, reviews the draft requirements, and sets the path forward. The next step is to secure a CPO briefing to understand their approval authority, decision timeline, and whether a competitive process (RFP/RFI) will be required.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c
SF Business_Requirements__c (null)
Avoma call 2026-03-24, Caryn Ochtman (buyer): '200-function Excel template,' 'draft business case,' 'what are we looking for?'
decision_criteria.narrative: CS Energy has begun articulating evaluation priorities but has not formalized decision criteria or weightings. Business_Requirements__c is null, but Customer_Business_Problem__c outlines four themes: replacing manual processes with a formal procurement system; strengthening compliance and governance controls; aligning procurement with ESG and sustainability goals; and securing modern, integrated technology with data security. Top_Challenges_Priorities__c is null (field does not exist in this org). On the 2026-01-20 discovery meeting (not recorded), attendees included the three known contacts plus Luke Dougherty and Akshay Dhanda from Zycus, suggesting an introduction-level discussion. On 2026-03-24, Caryn Ochtman referenced a '200-function Excel template' (functional requirements template) that Zycus provided, and stated the procurement leadership team would 'get together... going, well, what are we looking for?' before presenting to the new CPO. She added they might draft 'a draft business case going, hey, here's our thoughts' for the CPO to 'layer on top of.' This indicates requirements definition is in progress internally but incomplete and not yet validated with the new decision maker. No RFP, RFI, scorecard, or vendor comparison framework has been mentioned. The criteria exist in concept (functionality scope, compliance, ESG, security, ROI) but are not decision-ready. The next step is to facilitate requirements finalization with the CPO and position Zycus's S2P + AI capabilities (Merlin, Agentic AI) against those finalized criteria.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — AIS Score 4.0, AIS Status Moderate. AIS_Why__c states: 'Buyer is disengaged; AI is being pushed by rep, not pulled by buyer. Buyer language is all about CPO transition and example use cases - never AI, Merlin, or Agentic AI by name from a CS Energy stakeholder. Products in scope include Merlin + Agentic AI + Merlin Intake, but the buyer's stance is pushed/flustered not enthusiastic.' On the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn Ochtman described the procurement team as 'flustered with a gazillion things' and resource-constrained; when the rep offered to show AI-powered procurement capabilities, she did not engage on AI, instead deferring to the CPO onboarding. No AI-related language appears in Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, or buyer quotes. The tier is AI Curious at the low end: the operational pain (manual processes, compliance gaps) is a natural AI use case, and the product scope includes AI, but the buyer has not articulated AI appetite or urgency. Positioning AI as the solution to their stated pain (automation, governance, ESG tracking) is the wedge, but it has not been activated.
bullets.text: Losing on capability fit — AIS Score 4.0, AIS Status Moderate.

bullets.full: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning is weak and misaligned. Products in scope include Merlin, Agentic AI, and Merlin Intake (the full AI-powered procurement suite), but the buyer has not requested, discussed, or validated AI capabilities. AIS_Why__c explicitly flags: 'AI is being pushed by rep, not pulled by buyer. Caryn Ochtman never spoke AI/Merlin/agentic-related words' on the 2026-03-24 call. The rep offered to 'give Jeff a bit of a look at our capability and what we're doing in the AI powered procurement space,' but this demo has not occurred. Buyer language centers on 'example use cases,' 'functional requirements template,' and the CPO transition, never AI differentiation. For a deal with Merlin + Agentic AI in the product scope and an AIS score of 4.0 (Moderate), the absence of buyer-initiated AI conversation signals the AI value proposition has not landed. The move is to lead with AI differentiation (Merlin negotiation agents on spend data, Agentic AI workflow automation) when briefing the CPO, anchored to CS Energy's stated pain (manual processes, compliance automation, ESG tracking) and a peer reference (SEQWater AI-guided procurement go-live).
bullets.text: Buyer is leaning elsewhere — AI positioning is weak and misaligned.

bullets.full: No real champion yet — No active champion. Caryn Ochtman (procurement governance lead) is the closest advocate but her engagement has cooled (103 days since last contact, no reply to June outreach) and her influence is limited by the CPO's decision authority. She transparently explained the stall (team overwhelmed, CPO transition) but has not driven internal urgency, introduced Zycus to the CPO, or advanced requirements definition. Dee Agarwal and Allistair Johnston show low visibility. The incoming CPO (Jeff Green from News Corp) holds decision authority but has not engaged substantively despite rep outreach. Champion status is weak-to-none until the CPO relationship is established.
bullets.text: No real champion yet — No active champion.

bullets.full: No executive access yet — No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, and Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c are all null. No contact role carries the 'Economic Buyer' or 'Executive Sponsor' designation. The three known contacts are all procurement/tech operational roles: Caryn Ochtman (Senior Manager Procurement Governance & Technology), Dee Agarwal (Snr Contracts and Procurement Manager), Allistair Johnston (Technology Commercial Manager). On the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn referenced a new 'Head of Strategic Sourcing and Supply' (Jeff Green from the relevant stakeholder) joining mid-April 2026 and stated: 'the ultimate decision most probably will end up landing in their particular square space... I would want to be involved in that direction.' This positions Jeff Green as the likely Decision Maker for project approval, but his level of budget authority is unconfirmed. Compelling_Event__c states 'no budget is currently allocated' as of the field's last edit. No C-level sponsor (CFO, CPO, or procurement executive with P&L authority) has appeared on any call or task. The synthesis read shows budget authority resides with the incoming CPO, but that person has not yet engaged, and no conversation has occurred about approval process, spend threshold, or commercial timeline. Mapping and engaging the Economic Buyer is the single highest-priority move once the CPO onboards.
bullets.text: No executive access yet — No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged.

bullets.full: A competitor is pushing back — No competitor identified or evidenced. CS Energy is described as 'operating without a formal procurement system' (greenfield), and no vendor comparison, RFP process, or incumbent displacement has been mentioned across 349 days in Qualified. For a government-owned entity planning a major S2P transformation, 'no competition' likely signals pre-competitive stage (requirements not finalized, market scan not run) rather than sole-source award. Zycus has early access via Caryn Ochtman (procurement governance lead) but has not yet briefed the new CPO (decision maker). Competitive risk is unknown and unmanaged.
bullets.text: A competitor is pushing back — No competitor identified or evidenced.

However, none of these have been quantified with cost, headcount waste, cycle-time impact, or savings opportunity. On the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn Ochtman described the team as 'flustered with a gazillion things' and 'time poor,' confirming resource strain but offering no dollar figure. Compelling_Event__c mentions 'ROI justification' as a requirement but provides no baseline numbers. The deal sits in Qualified nearly a year (349 days) with comprehensive pain articulation but zero financial metrics, blocking a defensible business case. The next move is to secure a quantified pain and ROI conversation with the new CPO once onboarded, anchoring the business case to measurable impact (cost avoidance, FTE productivity, compliance risk reduction) before advancing to evaluation.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'At Risk' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by momentum 56 vs stage-expected 50; business case strength +0.30 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 56 (moving forward): Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial). Reduced because the most recent contact is stale (recency ×0.93).
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 53 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Security/procurement review underway.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 2 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 53 and momentum 56, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Buyer actively engaged this sweep (live pulse + buyer calls).

Products in scope include Merlin + Agentic AI + Merlin Intake, but the buyer's stance is pushed/flustered not enthusiastic.' On the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn Ochtman described the procurement team as 'flustered with a gazillion things' and resource-constrained; when the rep offered to show AI-powered procurement capabilities, she did not engage on AI, instead deferring to the CPO onboarding. No AI-related language appears in Customer_Business_Problem__c, Compelling_Event__c, or buyer quotes. The tier is AI Curious at the low end: the operational pain (manual processes, compliance gaps) is a natural AI use case, and the product scope includes AI, but the buyer has not articulated AI appetite or urgency. Positioning AI as the solution to their stated pain (automation, governance, ESG tracking) is the wedge, but it has not been activated.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 2025-07-21, qualified next day (2025-07-22), and remained in Qualified for 349 days. Early progress: NDA executed 2026-02-05, buyer committed to provide example use cases for demo (never delivered). CPO transition announced 2026-03-31 (new CPO starting mid-April 2026), parking all buyer-side activity. Last buyer engagement 2026-03-24 (Caryn Ochtman call, 103 days ago). On 2026-06-19, deal owner extended close date 7 months (2026-08-28 → 2027-03-30), reduced amount $100K ($350K → $250K), and reached out to new CPO (Jeff Green) via email; LinkedIn accepted but no substantive reply. Deal is in CPO-gated holding pattern with no advancement pathway until new leadership engages.

No decision timeline or forcing event. Buyer stated CPO will 'contemplate what have I inherited' and set direction; close date extended 7 months (2026-08-28 → 2027-03-30) with no commitment secured. Deal could slip indefinitely if CPO deprioritizes.

CS Energy is a Queensland Government-Owned Corporation; procurement may be subject to public-sector probity, tender requirements, or political oversight. On 2026-03-24 call, Caryn referenced 'the joys of working for a GOC' when government requests forced reprioritization. No visibility into procurement governance or mandate.
Buyer deliverable overdue 5 months: committed to send example use cases for demo (2026-02-05), never delivered. No re-engagement or explanation. Signals low buyer urgency or internal misalignment.

No competitor mapped after 349 days in Qualified. For a government entity planning S2P transformation, competitive process (RFP/RFI) is likely; Zycus has no intelligence on who else is in play or when the market scan will occur.

champion_strength.source: SF OpportunityContactRole; Avoma call 2026-03-24; SF Next_Step__c 2026-06-19 (CPO outreach, no reply)
champion_strength.summary: No active champion. Caryn Ochtman (procurement governance lead) is the closest advocate but her engagement has cooled (103 days since last contact, no reply to June outreach) and her influence is limited by the CPO's decision authority. She transparently explained the stall (team overwhelmed, CPO transition) but has not driven internal urgency, introduced Zycus to the CPO, or advanced requirements definition. Dee Agarwal and Allistair Johnston show low visibility. The incoming CPO (Jeff Green from News Corp) holds decision authority but has not engaged substantively despite rep outreach. Champion status is weak-to-none until the CPO relationship is established.

items.action: Secure introduction to new CPO (Jeff Green) via the deal owner's manager or executive sponsor, deliver AI-powered S2P capability briefing anchored to CS Energy's stated pain (manual processes, compliance gaps, ESG tracking), position Merlin negotiation agents and Agentic AI workflow automation as the differentiation, and confirm his stance on procurement transformation timeline and budget authority

items.expected_effect: Restart buyer engagement, map CPO's decision authority and timeline, position Zycus AI differentiation before competitive process opens, secure commitment to next milestone (requirements review or demo)

items.action: Re-engage the relevant stakeholder and Dee Agarwal with a light value-add touch: share SEQWater AI-guided procurement go-live case study (Queensland peer reference, relevant to GOC context), offer to facilitate requirements finalization using the 200-function template with a Zycus solutions consultant, and request introduction to the CPO or confirmation of his onboarding status

items.expected_effect: Re-open communication channel with operational sponsors, demonstrate continued value and support, position for CPO introduction

items.action: Quantify the business case: work with Caryn/Dee or directly with the CPO to build a tailored ROI model anchoring CS Energy's articulated pain (manual processes, compliance risk, ESG gaps, data fragmentation) to financial impact (FTE productivity savings, compliance cost avoidance, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement), leveraging Zycus's benchmarking data for utilities/public-sector entities

items.expected_effect: Create defensible business case to support CPO approval and budget request, differentiate Zycus on measurable value delivery

items.action: Map the Economic Buyer and approval chain: once CPO relationship is established, confirm his budget authority level, identify CFO or C-level sponsor if CPO requires upward approval (likely for a $250K+ S2P transformation in a GOC), and secure introduction or briefing to that Economic Buyer

items.expected_effect: De-risk budget approval, ensure commercial conversation occurs with the person who controls the spend, accelerate decision timeline

items.action: Surface the competitive landscape proactively: ask the CPO (or Caryn/Dee) whether a formal RFP/RFI process is planned, who else they are evaluating, and what the vendor selection timeline looks like; if no competitive process is underway, position Zycus as the preferred partner and offer a pilot or phased approach to de-risk the decision

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled significantly. Last buyer touch 2026-03-24 (103 days ago); rep reached out 2026-06-19 (16 days ago) with no buyer reply. Caryn Ochtman transparently explained the stall (CPO transition, team overwhelmed with government requests, 'headspace to deal with this requirement keeps getting pushed') but provided no re-engagement timeline. Deal owner extended close date 7 months and reduced amount $100K on 2026-06-19, signaling acceptance of delay. No CPO engagement confirmed despite announced mid-April 2026 start. Buyer deliverable (example use cases) overdue 5 months with no follow-up. Single-threaded to mid-level contacts; no C-level or economic-buyer touch. However, buyer has not canceled, deprioritized explicitly, or stated 'no longer pursuing'; the stall is attributed to external factors (CPO transition, resource constraints), not loss of interest. Cooling but not ghost; requires immediate executive-connect intervention to restart.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-07-21, qualified 2025-07-22 (349 days in Qualified). Close date slipped 241 days forward on 2026-06-19 (from 2026-08-28 to 2027-03-30), extending runway to 268 days. Amount reduced $100K same day (from $350K to $250K). Deal has been in holding pattern since late March 2026 (3.5+ months) awaiting new CPO (Jeff Green from News Corp) to onboard and set direction. Last genuine buyer engagement: 2026-03-24 call with Caryn Ochtman (103 days ago), who stated procurement team is 'flustered with a gazillion things' and 'our headspace to actually deal with this particular requirement has actually keeps getting pushed.' Rep reached out 2026-06-19 (16 days ago) but no buyer reply yet. Time-in-stage is artificially extended by leadership transition, not buyer disinterest, but forward pace requires CPO engagement to restart. At current zero-activity rate, deal will not advance before close date without intervention.
evidence: Last buyer touch: 2026-03-24 call, Caryn Ochtman stated team overwhelmed, parked procurement project until new CPO onboards (SF Next_Step__c, Avoma transcript)
New CPO (Jeff Green) announced mid-April 2026 start per 2026-03-31 Next Step; no confirmation he has started or engaged as of 2026-07-05
Rep outreach 2026-06-19 via email to the new CPO; LinkedIn accepted but no substantive reply (SF Task, Next_Step__c)
Close date extended 7 months (2026-08-28 → 2027-03-30) and amount reduced $100K on 2026-06-19, signaling deal owner acknowledges delay
NDA executed 2026-02-05; buyer committed to send example use cases for demo, never delivered (Next_Step_History__c)
No Avoma calls since 2026-03-24; no completed Tasks since 2026-06-19 email; no evidence CPO has engaged
north_star_verdict.headline: New CPO onboarding has stalled deal progression for 2.5 months; rep-initiated outreach on 2026-06-19 awaits buyer reply; 268 days to close provides recovery runway but sponsor has explicitly acknowledged inability to advance until leadership transition completes.

best_practice_check.summary: Deal is single-threaded to mid-level procurement contacts with no CPO or C-level engagement after 349 days in Qualified. No competitor mapped, no quantified ROI case, no decision timeline, no multi-threading to economic buyer. CPO transition has created 3.5-month buyer silence; rep outreach (2026-06-19) awaits reply. Forward progress blocked until CPO engages.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor identified or evidenced. CS Energy is described as 'operating without a formal procurement system' (greenfield), and no vendor comparison, RFP process, or incumbent displacement has been mentioned across 349 days in Qualified. For a government-owned entity planning a major S2P transformation, 'no competition' likely signals pre-competitive stage (requirements not finalized, market scan not run) rather than sole-source award. Zycus has early access via Caryn Ochtman (procurement governance lead) but has not yet briefed the new CPO (decision maker). Competitive risk is unknown and unmanaged.

items.quote: NDA executed, they will now send us 'example use cases' to demo back to senior Proc team
items.requirement: Provide example use cases for demo session to senior procurement team

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is weak and misaligned. Products in scope include Merlin, Agentic AI, and Merlin Intake (the full AI-powered procurement suite), but the buyer has not requested, discussed, or validated AI capabilities. AIS_Why__c explicitly flags: 'AI is being pushed by rep, not pulled by buyer. Caryn Ochtman never spoke AI/Merlin/agentic-related words' on the 2026-03-24 call. The rep offered to 'give Jeff a bit of a look at our capability and what we're doing in the AI powered procurement space,' but this demo has not occurred. Buyer language centers on 'example use cases,' 'functional requirements template,' and the CPO transition, never AI differentiation. For a deal with Merlin + Agentic AI in the product scope and an AIS score of 4.0 (Moderate), the absence of buyer-initiated AI conversation signals the AI value proposition has not landed. The move is to lead with AI differentiation (Merlin negotiation agents on spend data, Agentic AI workflow automation) when briefing the CPO, anchored to CS Energy's stated pain (manual processes, compliance automation, ESG tracking) and a peer reference (SEQWater AI-guided procurement go-live).

items.quote: CS Energy is operating without a formal procurement system... Manual processes, inefficiencies, high risk of errors, and poor visibility into spend.
items.criterion: Replace manual procurement processes with formal S2P system

items.quote: Needs better control and consistency in procurement governance... Risk of policy breaches, audit challenges, and lack of process standardization.
Looking to align procurement with environmental and social goals... Difficulty tracking supplier sustainability practices and measuring ESG impact.
items.criterion: Align procurement with ESG and sustainability goals

items.quote: Requires secure, modern systems that align with internal IT policies... Data fragmentation, lack of integration, and potential security risk.
items.criterion: Secure, modern, integrated technology with data security

items.quote: Luke (rep) offered: 'we can give Jeff a bit of a look at our capability and what we're doing in the AI powered procurement space.' Buyer is disengaged; AI is being pushed by rep, not pulled by buyer. Caryn Ochtman never spoke AI/Merlin/agentic-related words.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: CS Energy's articulated needs align well with Zycus's S2P + AI product scope, but fit has not been validated in a structured demo or evaluation. Customer_Business_Problem__c outlines four requirements: replace manual procurement processes with a formal system (Zycus S2P suite: iSource, iContract, iSupplier, iSave); strengthen compliance and governance (iContract workflow + audit trail); align procurement with ESG/sustainability goals (supplier management + reporting capabilities); and secure, integrated technology (iSaaS + data security). Products in scope include the full S2P suite plus Merlin (AI negotiation), Agentic AI, Merlin Intake, and iRisk, covering the stated needs. However, on the 2026-03-24 call, Caryn Ochtman acknowledged the procurement team had received Zycus's '200-function Excel template' (functional requirements tool) but had not yet completed it or reviewed it due to being 'time poor.' No demo of AI capabilities (Merlin, Agentic AI) has been delivered to the buyer; the rep offered to 'give Jeff a bit of a look at our capability and what we're doing in the AI powered procurement space' on the call, but this has not occurred. AIS_Why__c flags that AI is being 'pushed by rep, not pulled by buyer' and that 'Caryn Ochtman never spoke AI/Merlin/agentic-related words' on the call. Fit is strong on paper, unproven in practice, and not yet positioned to the decision maker.

hard.ais_why:
• On Mar 24 2026, Caryn Ochtman (HOP) said in her catch-up with Luke Dougherty: 'we just seem to be at the moment quite flustered with a gazillion things that we need to to to do, and... our our headspace to actually deal with this particular requirement has actually keeps getting pushed.' On the same call Luke (rep) offered: 'we can give Jeff a bit of a look at our capability and what we're doing in the AI powered procurement space.' Buyer is disengaged; AI is being pushed by rep, not pulled by buyer.
• Per the 31 Mar 2026 rep note from Luke Dougherty: 'New CPO starts mid April and team will present requirements / plans to him for decision to proceed.' Earlier 5 Feb: 'NDA executed, they will now send us example use cases to demo back to senior Proc team.' Buyer language is all about CPO transition and example use cases - never AI, Merlin, or Agentic AI by name from a CS Energy stakeholder.
• High confidence: 3 Avoma meetings (csenergy.com.au confirmed), full Mar 24 transcript reviewed - Caryn Ochtman never spoke AI/Merlin/agentic-related words. Products in scope include Merlin + Agentic AI + Merlin Intake, but the buyer's stance is 'pushed/flustered' not enthusiastic. Stage is Qualified with new-CPO transition gating - AI Curious at the low end.
